summary:
The lingual nerve is the most commonly injured structure during a mandibular nerve block, with reported injury rates reaching up to 23% in third molar extractions. Facial nerve injuries are rare and lack supporting clinical evidence specific to mandibular nerve blocks. 

Note: This summary reflects only what was stated in Agent1's final corrected reasoning after reviewing available literature. It does NOT include external information from outside agents' arguments unless clearly derived within their own analysis chain.
